name: Caves of Puente Viesgo and Vía Verde del Pas - round trip
name_original: Cuevas de Puente Viesgo y Vía Verde del Pas – ida y vuelta
region_intro: Touring cycling around Santiurde De Toranzo features routes that traverse river valleys and historical greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Pas. The terrain includes gentle gradients along waterways and more varied elevations leading to aqueducts and through rural landscapes. The region is characterized by its green valleys and the presence of historical structures, providing diverse backdrops for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Santiurde De Toranzo The most popular touring cycling route is Caves of Puente Viesgo and Vía Verde del Pas - round trip, a 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway and passes by notable cave systems. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Arroyal Aqueduct – Puente Viesgo loop from Alceda, a moderate 45.4 miles (73.0 km) path. This route offers a loop experience, passing by the El Arroyal Aqueduct and through the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Santiurde de Toranzo Aqueduct – El Arroyal Aqueduct loop from Puente Viesgo, a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through areas featuring aqueducts, often completed in about 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Santiurde De Toranzo is defined by river valleys, historical aqueducts, and converted railway greenways. The network offers options for easy family-friendly touring bike trails as well as more challenging routes with greater elevation changes. The routes in Santiurde De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santiurde De Toranzo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes available around Santiurde De Toranzo, catering to various skill levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 45 difficult routes for more experienced cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists appreciate most about the routes here?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santiurde De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which includes river valleys, historical greenways like the Vía Verde del Pas, and scenic paths leading to aqueducts.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: Yes, Santiurde De Toranzo offers 14 easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiurde de Toranzo Aqueduct – El Arroyal Aqueduct loop from Puente Viesgo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.8 km long and can be completed in under an hour.

Q: What are some more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 45 difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arroyal Aqueduct – Puente Viesgo loop from San Vicente de Toranzo&lt;/a&gt;, an 81.5 km route with significant elevation changes, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arroyal Aqueduct – Wooden bridge loop from Alceda&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 64.7 km.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Santiurde De Toranzo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012288176/caves-of-puente-viesgo-and-via-verde-del-pas-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Puente Viesgo and Vía Verde del Pas - round trip&lt;/a&gt;, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arroyal Aqueduct – Puente Viesgo loop from Alceda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: The region offers several family-friendly touring cycling routes, particularly those that follow the gentle gradients of river valleys and converted railway greenways like the Vía Verde del Pas. These routes provide a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the impressive aqueducts, or natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Capía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach touring cycling trailheads in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads can vary. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for services connecting to towns like Puente Viesgo or Alceda, which are common starting points for many routes in the Santiurde De Toranzo area.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for the routes, such as Puente Viesgo, Alceda, or San Vicente de Toranzo. Look for designated parking areas or public car parks within these communities.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santiurde De Toranzo?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Santiurde De Toranzo is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are lush. Summer can also be pleasant, though it may be warmer. Winter cycling is possible but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that follow historical greenways?
A: Yes, a key feature of touring cycling in Santiurde De Toranzo is the presence of historical greenways. The Vía Verde del Pas is a notable example, offering flat and scenic paths along a converted railway line, perfect for a smooth ride.
tips: It is located right next to the greenway. It was built in 1885 in order to supply drinking water to the city of Santander. It is surrounded by nature and there are chairs, tables and benches in the park.

German-made locomotive that entered service in 1913 to transport iron ores and limestone from the Camargo mines to its factory. It is located on the greenway, near the train station, converted into an exhibition hall and tourist information office.
The old Puente Viesgo train station has not lost any of its charm. It is also worth taking a break in the town. The necessary restaurants and bars are mainly located west of the river.

A very beautiful and picturesque town where it is impossible not to eat a sobao. There is a path to walk along the edge of the river but you will have to leave the bike behind. Tied up of course!

It also has a thermal complex where you can come and relax.

The set of the Cuevas del Monte Castillo in Puente Viesgo is undoubtedly one of the greatest exponents of Rock Art in Spain. It is a set of four cavities with Paleolithic rock art, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO in 2008. Of the four, two are open to the public: El Castillo and Las Monedas. You can buy tickets at cuevas.culturadecantabria.com
